hero.title: Every stat the scoreboard doesn't show you

feature1.subtitle: Built for competition
feature1.title: See what the scoreboard doesn't show
feature1.description: Parsertime captures the full picture — killfeeds, hero swaps, ultimate economy, and role-specific performance — so you can coach with data, not guesswork.

builtByCoaches.name: Scrim overview at a glance
builtByCoaches.description: See map results, match time, first pick rates, and more on a single card.

shareWithTeam.name: Your whole team, one place
shareWithTeam.description: Invite players and coaches to a shared workspace where everyone sees the same data.

advancedStatistics.name: Advanced statistics
advancedStatistics.description: Gain insight into your players' performance over time. Track their stats and see how they improve. See trends and patterns week over week.

feature2.subtitle: Everything you need
feature2.title: Scrim stats at your fingertips
feature2.description: See your stats after your scrims instead of only relying on replay codes. Our platform allows you to keep your data no matter what happens to your replays.

dataCharts.name: Rich data visualizations
dataCharts.description: Charts, tables, and breakdowns that surface the stats that matter most to your coaching.

filterStats.name: Track trends over time
filterStats.description: Filter by week, month, or season to see how your team is progressing and where to focus next.

userProfiles.name: User profiles.
userProfiles.description: See individual player stats and trends over time. Understand how your players are improving and where they need help.

advancedSecurity.name: Private by default
advancedSecurity.description: Passwordless login, team-scoped permissions, and zero data sharing. Your scrims stay yours.

fastReliable.name: Fast and reliable.
fastReliable.description: We're built on top of the latest cloud infrastructure. Speed is never an issue. We're always up and running.

fullyCustomizable.name: Fully customizable.
fullyCustomizable.description: Need custom functionality or want to track specific metrics? Let us know and we'll make it happen.

stats.subtitle: By the numbers
stats.title: Backed by real data
stats.description: Parsertime processes thousands of scrims for teams competing at the highest level.

testimonial.quote: “In my 8 years of coaching, I have used many different tools for analysis but this is the one I wish I had all along. Parsertime has been a game changer for us by identified trends and metrics that would otherwise be overlooked with just coaches watching alone. Both the ability to track team improvement incrementally and developing scouting plans based on scrim data make this tool a must for any serious coaches and teams.”
testimonial.author: DSG byZenith (@byZenith)

cta.subtitle: Your scrims hold the answers.
cta.title: Start coaching with data today.
cta.description: Create a team, upload your first scrim, and see results in minutes. Free to get started — no credit card required.

howItWorks.title: Get started in
howItWorks.step1Description: Sign up and invite your players and coaches. Everyone gets access to the same dashboard — no setup required on their end.
howItWorks.step2Description: Drop in your scrim data after each session. Our parser handles the heavy lifting and processes everything automatically.
howItWorks.step3Description: Charts, trends, and player ratings are available immediately. Identify what to work on before your next practice.

csrSpotlight.subtitle: Custom Hero Skill Rating
csrSpotlight.title: Know exactly where you stand
csrSpotlight.description: Our proprietary CSR system rates every player on a 1–5000 scale using statistical Z-scores across role-specific metrics. See your tier badge, track your improvement, and compete on the leaderboard.
csrSpotlight.highlight1Description: Individual skill ratings for every hero you play, weighted by role-specific stats like eliminations, deaths, and healing.
csrSpotlight.highlight2Description: From Bronze to Champion — see your rank at a glance with visually distinct tier badges.
csrSpotlight.highlight3Description: Understand where you fall in the player population with bell curve distribution charts and percentile rankings.

teamStatsSpotlight.subtitle: Team Analytics Dashboard
teamStatsSpotlight.title: Eight dimensions of team performance
teamStatsSpotlight.description: Dive deep into your team's performance with dedicated tabs for Overview, Performance, Heroes, Trends, Maps, Swaps, Teamfights, and Ultimates.
teamStatsSpotlight.highlight1Description: Radar charts, heatmaps, trend lines, and map galleries that make patterns obvious at a glance.
teamStatsSpotlight.highlight2Description: Compare Tank, Damage, and Support performance with normalized radar charts and find your team's strengths.
teamStatsSpotlight.highlight3Description: Track your team's trajectory week over week with trend lines that show exactly what's working.

avgUltChargeTime.footer: The average time it takes to build an ultimate. A lower time is better, while a longer time might indicate that you are not doing enough damage or healing.

avgTimeUseUlt.footer: The average time it takes to use an ultimate after it's charged. A longer time might indicate that you are not using your ultimate effectively or are holding onto it for too long.

avgDroughtTime.title: Average Drought Time
avgDroughtTime.footer: The drought time is the average time between your most recent kill and your next kill. A high drought time can indicate that you are not participating in fights as often as you should be.

xFactor.title: X Factor
xFactor.footer: The X Factor is a measure of a player's individual impact on a fight. If your X Factor is high, you are likely to be the deciding factor in a fight. Please note that this is calculated differently for each role.

dmgTakenHealingReceived.title: Damage Taken vs Healing Received
dmgTakenHealingReceived.footer: This chart shows the cumulative damage taken and healing received by round. The x-axis represents the round number, and the y-axis represents the cumulative damage taken or healing received.

dmgDoneDmgTaken.title: Hero Damage Dealt vs Damage Taken
dmgDoneDmgTaken.footer: This chart shows the hero damage dealt and damage taken by round. The x-axis represents the round number, and the y-axis represents the damage dealt or damage taken.

mvpScore.title: MVP Score
mvpScore.footer: The MVP score is a calculation based on a player's statistics compared to the averages of all other players who have played the same hero in scrims. Hover over this card to see how the MVP score is calculated.
mvpScore.percentile: {percentile, selectordinal, one {#st} two {#nd} few {#rd} other {#th}} percentile
mvpScore.explanation: The MVP score can be used to understand how a player is performing compared to similar players on their hero. The MVP stat does not take into account a player's performance compared to their teammates—it is a way to see who is having a stand-out performance compared to the global averages on their hero. MVP scores typically range between -100 and +100, with a normal distribution centered around 0.

dmgByRound.title: Hero Damage By Round
dmgByRound.footer: This chart shows the hero damage done by round for {playerName}. The x-axis represents the round, and the y-axis represents the damage done. Note that the damage is NOT cumulative, so the damage done in round 2 does not include the damage done in round 1.

dmgBlockByRound.title: Damage Mitigated By Round

analysis.title: Analysis
analysis.footerDeaths: First deaths are determined by the first kill in each fight. A fight is a cluster of kills within 15 seconds of each other. The team that suffers the first death in a fight is at a disadvantage.
analysis.footerUltimates: Ultimate usage is tracked from in-game ultimate activation events. "Used ult first" means which team activated an ultimate of that role first in each fight. Fight-opening ultimates are the first ultimate used in a fight window.
analysis.footerTiming: Ult timing categorizes each ultimate as initiation (first third of fight), midfight (middle third), or late (final third). Players are assigned to subroles based on their most-played heroes.
analysis.footerEfficiency: Efficiency rating = fights won per ultimate used. Wasted ultimates are those used when the team was already down 3+ players. Dry fights are fights where no ultimates were used. Reversals are fights won after being down 2+ kills.
analysis.footerRotationDeaths: Rotation deaths are kills that occur before teams engage — typically when a player is picked off while the team is moving between positions. Detected by checking if the first kill in a fight happens while team centroids are more than 50 meters apart.
analysis.footerSwaps: Hero swaps are tracked from in-game hero change events. Round-start utility swaps (e.g. swapping to Lúcio for speed boost) are filtered out to show only mid-round strategic swaps.

charts.tooltip: Looking for more information on charts? <link>Check out the documentation</link> to learn more.

killsByFight.title: Kills By Fight
killsByFight.description: Kills are grouped by 15 second intervals. This chart shows the cumulative kills for each team at each interval. The x-axis represents the time in seconds, and the y-axis represents the cumulative kills. Team 1 is represented with positive numbers, while Team 2 is represented with negative numbers. overview.weightedWinRateTooltip: Win rate weighted by recency — recent matches count more than older ones.
overview.matchHistoryDescription: All recorded matches, most recent first
overview.noMatches: No matches found for this team.

points.0: Win rate is calculated from all recorded OWCS matches for this team.
points.1: Weighted win rate uses exponential decay with a 90-day half-life — a match from 90 days ago counts half as much as one from today.
points.2: Recent form shows the last 10 match results in chronological order, most recent first. The sparkline shows the win/loss trajectory over those matches.
points.3: Strength Rating is an Elo-style score centered at 1500. Beating higher-rated opponents raises it more than beating lower-rated ones. Below 5 matches the rating is marked as provisional.
points.4: Record Quality counts how many wins came against teams rated above 1500 (the starting average), showing whether a strong record was earned against strong competition.
points.5: Match data is sourced from Liquipedia and covers the last year of OWCS tournaments. When you select your team with the picker, your scrim data is also factored in.

heroBans.bansAgainstDescription: Heroes opponents ban when facing this team — reveals perceived strengths.
heroBans.bansByDescription: Heroes this team bans — reveals what they want to deny opponents.

points.0: Each OWCS map may include one hero ban per team. We track which heroes are banned by and against this team across all maps.
points.1: Weighted counts apply exponential decay (90-day half-life) so recent ban patterns are prioritized over older ones.
points.2: Disruption Score measures how much banning a hero hurts the opponent. It combines the win-rate delta (their WR when the hero is available minus when it's banned) with sample reliability. A score above 10 is significant; above 20 is a high-impact ban target.
points.3: Win-Rate Delta (pp) is in percentage points. For example, '−22pp when banned' means the opponent's win rate drops 22 percentage points when that hero is unavailable. Deltas above ±15pp are notable; above ±25pp are extreme.
points.4: Their Ban Targets cross-references the opponent's ban history with your most-played heroes. Critical: they ban it 30%+ of maps and your team plays it 10%+ of the time. Watch: ban rate 15%+ and play rate 5%+. Safe: low overlap.
points.5: Protected Heroes are heroes this team bans in fewer than 5% of their maps — these are composition staples they want available.

methodology.title: How we measure map performance and matchups

points.0: Win rate per map is computed from individual map results within each series, not just series wins.
points.1: Strength-weighted win rate adjusts for opponent quality using Elo ratings. A 60% WR against top-rated teams is worth more than 80% against bottom-rated teams. When no Elo data exists, raw WR is shown instead.
points.2: Net advantage (pp) is the percentage-point gap between your map WR and the opponent's strength-weighted WR. Scale: ±10pp is a minor edge, ±20pp is a clear advantage, ±30pp+ is a dominant matchup. Only matchups with ≥15pp gap appear in the veto recommendation.
points.3: Trend arrows compare the last 10 maps to the overall record for each map. A delta above +10pp is 'improving' (↑, shown in red — caution), below −10pp is 'declining' (↓, shown in green — opportunity), otherwise 'stable' (→).
points.4: Confidence dots indicate sample size: filled green = 20+ maps (high), amber = 10–19 (medium), faded = 5–9 (low). Matchups where either side has fewer than 5 maps are hidden from the veto recommendation.
points.5: When you select your team via the picker, your scrim map data is combined with the opponent's OWCS data. Both are decay-weighted identically; scrim data uses stricter confidence thresholds (30/15/7 maps instead of 20/10/5).

recommendations.title: Strategic Recommendations
recommendations.description: Actionable insights based on recency-weighted analysis of this team's performance.
recommendations.heroesToBanDescription: Heroes opponents most frequently ban against this team — these are their perceived strengths.
recommendations.mapsToPickDescription: Maps where this team has the lowest weighted win rate — exploit these weaknesses.
recommendations.mapsToAvoidDescription: Maps where this team has the highest weighted win rate — stay away from their strengths.
recommendations.noRecommendations: Not enough data to generate recommendations.

points.0: Heroes to Ban are the heroes most frequently banned against this team by their opponents — banning these denies their perceived strengths.
points.1: Maps to Pick are maps where this team has the lowest recency-weighted win rate (minimum 3 games played). Picking these exploits their weaknesses.
points.2: Maps to Avoid are maps where this team has the highest recency-weighted win rate. Avoid giving them home-field advantage.
points.3: Confidence levels are based on sample size: High (10+ games), Medium (5-9 games), Low (under 5 games).
points.4: All metrics use a 90-day half-life exponential decay, so recommendations reflect the current meta rather than outdated results.

points.0: This tab shows YOUR roster's readiness against this specific opponent. It identifies which of your players are most at risk based on their hero depth and how this opponent's ban patterns target them.
points.1: Z-scores measure how a player's per-10-minute stats on each hero compare to all hero performances in the same role across your roster. 0σ = role average, +1.5σ+ = standout, −0.5σ = below average.
points.2: Stats weighted by role — Tank: deaths (30% inverted), elims (20%), damage (20%). Damage: elims (30%), damage (30%), deaths (20% inverted). Support: healing (35%), deaths (25% inverted), damage (14%), elims (10%).
points.3: Primary/secondary delta is the z-score gap between a player's best and second-best hero. A delta above 1.5σ means a significant drop-off when forced off their main — they become a ban target for this opponent.
points.4: Vulnerability Index = hero depth delta × opponent ban rate. Scale: above 0.5 = critical (large drop-off + frequently banned), 0.25–0.5 = high, 0.1–0.25 = moderate, below 0.1 = low.
points.5: Heroes need at least 3 maps with 2+ minutes of play time to be included. Players need at least 5 total maps for a meaningful confidence rating.

points.0: Insights are generated by cross-referencing your team's uploaded scrim data with the opponent's competitive OWCS match history. Selecting your team with the picker enables the full cross-reference.
points.1: Map matchup insights surface when the net advantage between your WR and the opponent's strength-weighted WR exceeds ±15pp. Scale: 15–25pp is a clear edge, 25–40pp is a major mismatch, 40pp+ is dominant.
points.2: Ban disruption insights flag heroes where banning causes the opponent's win rate to drop significantly. A disruption score above 10 is notable; above 20 is a high-priority ban.
points.3: Player vulnerability insights flag players whose primary hero is both significantly better than their secondary (>1.5σ delta) and frequently banned by the opponent (>15% ban rate).
points.4: Each insight has a priority score (1–100) combining magnitude of advantage, confidence level, and actionability. Only the top 5 are featured; the rest appear in 'Additional Findings.'
points.5: Confidence tiers — High: 20+ maps. Medium: 10–19. Low: 5–9. Insufficient (hidden): under 5. Scrim-only data uses stricter thresholds (30/15/7) since practice matches carry lower signal strength.
points.6: Data source is shown on each insight card: 'Competitive' = OWCS matches only, 'Scrim' = your uploaded scrim data only, 'Competitive + Scrim' = both sources combined.

scrimOverview.title: Scrim Performance
scrimOverview.description: Key metrics from Parsertime scrim data.

methodology.mvpScore: Points-based score comparing per-10 stats against global hero averages. Each stat contributes up to ±10 points weighted by importance (4 pts per standard deviation). Range is typically −100 to +100.
methodology.kdRatio: Total eliminations divided by total deaths across all recorded scrims. Eliminations include both final blows and assists.
methodology.firstPickRate: Percentage of team fights where this player scored the opening kill. Measured per-map and averaged across all maps.
methodology.firstDeathRate: Percentage of team fights where this player was the first to die. A high rate may indicate aggressive positioning or being targeted.
methodology.fightReversalRate: Percentage of fights won by this player's team after losing 2 or more players first. Indicates clutch potential.
methodology.killsPerUltimate: Average number of eliminations achieved per ultimate ability used. Higher values suggest efficient ultimate usage.
methodology.consistencyScore: Scored 0–100 based on how stable per-10 stats are across maps. Calculated as the inverse of the average coefficient of variation for eliminations, deaths, damage, and healing. Above 75 is highly consistent.
methodology.mapsPlayed: Total number of scrim maps recorded in Parsertime for this player. Serves as the sample size for all scrim-based metrics.

performanceRadar.title: Performance Profile
performanceRadar.description: Z-scores vs global hero averages across all Parsertime players.
performanceRadar.noData: No scrim data available for radar chart.
performanceRadar.methodology: Each axis shows how many standard deviations (σ) this player's per-10 stat is from the global average for their hero. Calculated using baselines from all Parsertime players on the same hero, across at least 3 maps of data.

heroZScores.title: Hero Performance
heroZScores.description: Composite z-scores per hero compared to global baselines.
heroZScores.methodology: Composite z-score is the weighted average of per-stat z-scores (eliminations, deaths, damage, healing, blocked) for each hero. Individual stat z-scores compare this player's per-10 rate to the global mean and standard deviation for that hero. The delta shows the gap between primary and secondary hero proficiency.

mapWinrates.title: Map Performance
mapWinrates.methodologyMapType: Win rate by map type is calculated from all competitive match results where this player's team participated. Percentages are wins ÷ total maps played for each game mode (Control, Escort, Hybrid, Push, Flashpoint).
mapWinrates.methodologyByMap: Individual map win rates are calculated from competitive match results. Borders are color-coded: green for ≥60% win rate, red for ≤40%, and neutral otherwise.

killAnalysis.title: Combat Patterns
killAnalysis.description: Kill and death analysis from scrim data.
killAnalysis.methodology: Kill and death data is sourced from Parsertime scrim logs. Top targets and threats show the 5 most-eliminated and most-died-to heroes by total count. Kill methods are grouped by ability type. Role distribution reflects the percentage of playtime on Tank, Damage, and Support based on hero time across all scrims.

strengthsWeaknesses.title: Scouting Insights
strengthsWeaknesses.description: Auto-generated strengths and weaknesses from competitive and scrim data.
strengthsWeaknesses.noStrengths: Not enough data to identify strengths.
strengthsWeaknesses.methodology: Insights are auto-generated by analyzing z-scores (hero performance vs global averages), competitive map win rates, first pick/death rates, consistency scores, and hero pool depth. Strengths trigger when metrics exceed positive thresholds; weaknesses trigger on negative thresholds. All data sources are combined to provide a holistic view.

hero.title: Born from the scrim room
hero.subtitle: Parsertime started as an internal tool for one collegiate Overwatch team. Two years and hundreds of thousands of stats later, it’s the analytics platform coaches actually want to open after practice.

story.title: How it started
story.paragraph1: In November 2023, the coaching staff at FIU Panthers had a problem: after every scrim, reviewing player stats meant copying numbers into spreadsheets, squinting at columns, and hoping someone didn’t fat-finger a formula. There had to be a better way.
story.paragraph2: Parsertime launched publicly in April 2024. Within hours, teams outside FIU were signing up. Feature requests poured in. A simple stat viewer grew into a full analytics suite—skill ratings, trend lines, team dashboards, a Discord bot, and an open dataset used by the community for research.
story.paragraph3: The goal hasn’t changed since that first commit: make it effortless for coaches and players to turn raw scrim data into insight they can act on before the next practice.

milestone1.title: First commit
milestone1.description: Development kicks off with a single goal: parse Overwatch scrim logs and display per-player stats without a spreadsheet in sight.

milestone2.title: Public beta
milestone2.description: Parsertime opens to all teams. 160 visitors on launch day, with the first external teams signing up within hours.

milestone3.title: v1.1 — Out of beta
milestone3.description: Player Stats, Hero Stats, and a Global Leaderboard ship. The beta label comes off. Paid plans go live via Stripe.

milestone4.title: v2.0 — Composite Skill Ratings
milestone4.description: The flagship CSR system launches: Z-score-based ratings on a 1–5,000 scale, hero-specific tiers, MVP scores, achievements, and a community leaderboard.

milestone5.title: v2.1 — Discord bot & scouting
milestone5.description: A Discord bot, team performance simulator, player scouting analytics, and a redesigned landing page ship in the biggest update yet.

stats.subtitle: By the numbers
stats.title: The data speaks for itself
stats.description: Every number here is live from the database—updated the moment a team uploads a scrim.

values.title: What drives every commit
values.subtitle: Six principles that shape the roadmap, the UI, and every line of code.

convenience.name: Convenience
convenience.description: Upload a scrim, see results. No spreadsheets, no manual entry, no waiting. Answers should be seconds away, not hours.

accessibility.name: Accessibility
accessibility.description: First-time coaches and seasoned analysts should both feel at home. If it takes a tutorial to understand, it’s too complicated.

community.name: Community first
community.description: Built by an Overwatch player, for Overwatch players. The community’s feature requests shape the roadmap—the Discord bot exists because it was the most-requested feature.

openSource.name: Open source
openSource.description: Every line of Parsertime’s code is public. Browse it, fork it, open a PR—the best tools are built in the open.

improvement.name: Continuous improvement
improvement.description: Seven major releases in two years. If something can be better, it ships in the next update—not the next quarter.

reliable.name: Reliable and performant
reliable.description: 99.99% uptime and fast load times, because the five minutes after a scrim ends are when the data matters most.

founder.label: The Founder
founder.name: Lucas Doell
founder.bio: Lucas built Parsertime because he got tired of copying scrim stats into Google Sheets for FIU Panthers. What started as a side project turned into a full analytics platform—and he still ships code, reviews community PRs, and argues about hero balance on Discord.

openSource.title: Open source from day one
openSource.description: Parsertime’s source code has been public since the first commit. Browse the codebase, report a bug, or submit a pull request—contributions from the community make the platform better for everyone.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Can't find the answer you're looking for? Reach out to our <link>support</link> team.
faq.question1: Do you offer discounts for collegiate teams?
faq.answer1: Yes. Reach out to our support team and we’ll set you up.
faq.question2: Do you offer discounts for Calling All Heroes teams?
faq.answer2: Yes — we want to support the CAH community. Contact our support team and we’ll work something out.
faq.question3: How can I manage my subscription?
faq.answer3: Manage your subscription in your <link>account settings</link>. If anything looks off, our support team can help.
faq.question4: Can I cancel my subscription at any time?
faq.answer4: Yes, cancel anytime. You’ll keep access through the end of your billing period.
faq.question5: What if I need higher limits than the Premium plan offers?
faq.answer5: Need more than 10 teams or 20 members? Contact our <link>support team</link> and we’ll put together a custom plan.
faq.answer3Plain: Manage your subscription in your account settings. If anything looks off, our support team can help.
faq.answer5Plain: Need more than 10 teams or 20 members? Contact our support team and we’ll put together a custom plan.
faq.question6: What happens to my data if I downgrade?
faq.answer6: Your data stays safe. If you downgrade, you lose access to extended stat history timeframes, but nothing gets deleted. Upgrade again and it’s all still there.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.description: At Parsertime, we are committed to maintaining the trust and confidence of our visitors to our web site. In particular, we want you to know that Parsertime is not in the business of selling, renting, or trading email lists with other companies and businesses for marketing purposes. In this Privacy Policy, we've provided detailed information on when and why we collect your personal information, how we use it, and how we keep it secure.

collectInformation.title: Information We Collect
collectInformation.description: As part of the registration process for our web app, we collect personal information. We use that information for a couple of reasons: to tell you about stuff you've asked us to tell you about; to contact you if we need to obtain or provide additional information; to check our records are right and to check every now and then that you're happy and satisfied. Here are the types of information we collect:

email.title: Email Addresses
email.description: Your email address is required during the registration process to help with account creation, authentication, and necessary communication related to the functionality of the app. We do not use your email address for marketing purposes.

picture.title: Profile Pictures and Team Images
picture.description: You can upload a profile picture and team images. These images are used to personalize your account and team profiles within our application.

data.title: Game Logs and Performance Data
data.description: Overwatch 2 to visualize stats and show data on user and team performance. This data is strictly used to provide services offered by Parsertime and does not include any personally sensitive information.

useInformation.title: How We Use Your Information
useInformation.list2: Improve your experience on our site through personalization;
useInformation.list3: Communicate important notices, such as changes to our terms, conditions, and policies;
useInformation.list4: Address technical issues and protect our site from fraud and abuse.

storageSecurity.title: Storage and Security
storageSecurity.description: We take precautions to protect your information. When you submit sensitive information via the website, your information is protected both online and offline. Only employees who need the information to perform a specific job (for example, billing or customer service) are granted access to personally identifiable information.

thirdPartyLinks.title: Third-Party Links
thirdPartyLinks.description: Our website may include links to third-party websites. We do not have control over, and are not responsible for, the content or privacy practices of these sites. We encourage our users to be aware when they leave our site and to read the privacy statements of any other site that collects personally identifiable information.

changePrivacyPolicy.title: Changes to Our Privacy Policy
changePrivacyPolicy.description: We may update this policy from time to time in order to reflect, for example, changes to our practices or for other operational, legal, or regulatory reasons.

contactUs.title: Contact Us
contactUs.description: For more information about our privacy practices, if you have questions, or if you would like to make a complaint, please contact us by email at <link>privacy@lux.dev</link>.
